seed_problem: Place watchtowers on a forest graph to cover every cell within radius r, minimizing total cost; score = baseline_cost / achieved_cost.
mutation: composite: keep the graph-radius covering goal but (input) replace the pure grid with a grid-plus-express-corridor weighted graph and (output) attach a heterogeneous menu of candidate stations, each with an independent construction cost AND its own coverage radius, so the task becomes a NP-hard weighted set-cover / facility-location optimization instead of a uniform-cost k-center placement.
title: Harbor Watch: Minimum-Cost Surveillance of a Container Port
strategies: build-everything baseline: construct all candidate stations (guaranteed feasible, defines B)
weighted greedy set cover: repeatedly build the station minimizing cost per newly-monitored demand block, using graph-radius coverage
greedy + redundancy pruning: after a greedy cover, drop any station whose demand is fully covered by the others (cheapest-first)
randomized multi-restart greedy with local swaps: perturb greedy tie-breaks, prune redundancy each restart, and keep the cheapest feasible cover found
